scores.brief_assessment: Highly concrete and well-structured reference documenting proprietary Grove internals (directory layout, Expect.js API signatures, ellipsis patterns) that an agent can apply directly when creating JavaScript examples, though it only covers one of the six languages the parent skill supports and lightly redundantly lists method names already visible in the preceding code block.
scores.novel_info: The most novel details are the proprietary `Expect.js` comparison library and its full API — specifically the chained method signatures (`that()`, `withIgnoredFields()`, `withOrderedSort()`, `withUnorderedSort()`, `shouldMatch()`, `shouldResemble()`, `withSchema()`), the ellipsis pattern syntax used in expected output files (`"..."`, `"prefix..."`, `["..."]`, standalone `...`, `{ ... }`), and the `shouldResemble()`/`withSchema()` schema validation options (`count`, `required_fields`, `field_values`) — none of which correspond to any public npm package. A reviewer should verify these API signatures, method constraints (e.g., the mutual exclusivity rules), and ellipsis-matching behaviors against the actual source at `code-example-tests/javascript/driver/utils/Expect.js`.
